name: mc-dp-icons
description: A polished, Minecraft-inspired icon suite for the modern developer's workflow.

mc-dp-icons.workspaceDetection.title: Enable Workspace Detection
mc-dp-icons.workspaceDetection.markdownDescription:
If enabled, the extension scans your workspace for Minecraft project files (`pack.mcmeta`, Bedrock `manifest.json`, Beet or JMC config files) and automatically applies the `mc-dp-icons` theme if detected. If not, it switches back to your `#mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme#`.

This scan runs on startup and when modifying files.

mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme.title: Fallback Icon Theme
mc-dp-icons.fallbackIconTheme.markdownDescription:
*Requires `#mc-dp-icons.workspaceDetection#` to be enabled.*

Enter the exact ID of the icon theme you want to use for non-Minecraft workspaces (e.g., `vs-seti` or `material-icon-theme`).

If blank, the extension defaults to the icon theme that was active before `mc-dp-icons` was applied.

mc-dp-icons.dynamicFunctionIcons.title: Dynamic Function Icons
mc-dp-icons.dynamicFunctionIcons.markdownDescription:
If enabled, functions listed in your `tick.json` and `load.json` files automatically receive repeating and impulse command block icons.

Disable this to manually assign these icons using the name lists below.

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.loadFunctionNames.title: Load function names
mc-dp-icons.loadFunctionNames.markdownDescription:
Assign custom command block icons to specific function files using the lists below.

*Tip: Quickly add files to these lists by right-clicking a `.mcfunction` file in the Explorer and using the `Datapack Icons: Set Icon` context menu.*

For maximum precision, specify the direct parent folder (e.g., `subfolder/file`). [GlobPatterns](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api#GlobPattern) are also supported to match multiple files (e.g., `*_setup` or `phase_[0-9]`).

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.subfolderIcons.title: Enable Subfolder Icons
mc-dp-icons.subfolderIcons.markdownDescription:
*Requires a `pack.mcmeta` file at the root of the pack.*

If enabled, a workaround activates that allows JSON files nested inside category subfolders to receive their specific icon rather than a generic one (e.g., `data/namespace/item_modifier/subfolder/file.json` receives the diamond pickaxe icon). *Not recommended for extremely large workspaces.*

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.namespaceIcons.title: Namespace Icons
mc-dp-icons.namespaceIcons.markdownDescription:
*Requires a `pack.mcmeta` file at the root of the pack.*

If enabled, namespace folders (directories directly within `data` and `assets`) receive an ender chest icon.

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.overlayIcons.title: Overlay Icons
mc-dp-icons.overlayIcons.markdownDescription:
If enabled, overlay folders registered in your `pack.mcmeta` receive a glass chest icon.

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.christmasIcons.title: Use Christmas icons
mc-dp-icons.christmasIcons.markdownDescription:
Determines when chest icons switch to their festive Christmas variants.

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.hideFolderArrows.title: Hide folder arrows
mc-dp-icons.hideFolderArrows.markdownDescription:
If enabled, hides the expand/collapse arrows next to folders in the VS Code Explorer.

*Warning: This feature is not currently supported on VS Code Web.*

mc-dp-icons.debug.title: Enable debug logging
mc-dp-icons.debug.markdownDescription: If enabled, verbose debug messages are printed to the console.

commands.title: Datapack Icons: Set as Crowned Tick
Datapack Icons: Set as Crowned Load

scripts.check: bun run generate && tsc --noEmit -p ./ && bunx eslint src --ext ts
